title: rubia pseudogalium
intro: Rubia pseudogalium, also known as the Indian madder or woodland madder, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Rubia pseudogalium include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and improve immune function. The herbal preparations of Rubia pseudogalium include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the roots or leaves in hot water.
